# backend/core/audit_chain.py
# auto-generated explainer · 2026-06-11T10:01:42Z

## Purpose
Tamper-evident audit log HMAC chain · Iter 29 · C7 simplified closure.

Per §38.3 + §47.7: each audit row carries an HMAC computed over
(prev_hmac + content). Operator can replay the chain to detect any
row insertion · deletion · or modification.

Per §57.7: in-memory chain for dev · production wires to Postgres +
secret rotation.

## Imports
- from `__future__` import annotations
- from `_` import hashlib
- from `_` import hmac
- from `_` import json
- from `_` import os
- from `_` import time
- from `typing` import Any

## Inputs
- Env vars: INSUR_AUDIT_CHAIN_SECRET

## Process
### Functions
- `_hmac_for(prev_hmac, content, secret)`: (no docstring)
- `append(content, secret)`: Append a new audit row · returns the chain entry.
- `verify_chain(secret)`: Replay chain and report tamper status.
- `list_chain(limit)`: (no docstring)
- `reset_for_test()`: (no docstring)

## Outputs
- Return values from functions above

## Notable inline comments
- In-memory chain state · ((index, ts, hmac, content_json), ...)
